channel.title: AI Security Paper Digest
channel.description: Curated papers on AI security from ArXiv and ACL

item.title: BEAT: Visual Backdoor Attacks on VLM-based Embodied Agents via Contrastive Trigger Learning
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces BEAT, a framework to inject visual backdoors into VLM-based embodied agents using objects in the environment as triggers that cause persistent attacker-specified multi-step policies.
  • Proposes a two-stage training pipeline: supervised fine-tuning (SFT) across diverse scenes/placements, followed by Contrastive Trigger Learning (CTL) which formulates trigger discrimination as preference learning to sharpen decision boundaries.
  • Demonstrates high attack success (up to 80%) while preserving benign performance, shows generalization to out-of-distribution trigger placements, and reports that CTL substantially outperforms naive SFT under limited backdoor data.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Qiusi Zhan', 'Hyeonjeong Ha', 'Rui Yang', 'Sirui Xu', 'Hanyang Chen', 'Liang-Yan Gui', 'Yu-Xiong Wang', 'Huan Zhang', 'Heng Ji', 'Daniel Kang']
  • Tags: ['visual backdoor', 'VLM security', 'embodied agent attacks', 'contrastive learning', 'adversarial attack']

item.title: GRILL: Restoring Gradient Signal in Ill-Conditioned Layers for More Effective Adversarial Attacks on Autoencoders
item.description:
Summary

  • Identifies a failure mode in white-box adversarial attacks on autoencoders caused by vanishing adversarial-loss gradients through ill-conditioned layers (near-zero Jacobian singular values).
  • Proposes GRILL, a method to locally restore gradient signals in ill-conditioned layers to enable stronger norm-bounded (sample-specific and universal) attacks.
  • Demonstrates across multiple AE architectures and adaptive attack settings that GRILL substantially increases attack effectiveness and reveals greater vulnerability in AE robustness evaluations.
  • Provides empirical evidence that encoder–decoder components in modern multimodal architectures exhibit similar vulnerabilities exploitable by GRILL.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Chethan Krishnamurthy Ramanaik', 'Arjun Roy', 'Tobias Callies', 'Eirini Ntoutsi']
  • Tags: ['adversarial attacks', 'autoencoders', 'gradient manipulation', 'robustness evaluation', 'multimodal vulnerabilities']

item.title: BLM-Guard: Explainable Multimodal Ad Moderation with Chain-of-Thought and Policy-Aligned Rewards
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes BLM-Guard, an explainable multimodal ad moderation framework combining chain-of-thought reasoning, rule-based policy principles, and a critic-guided reward for policy alignment.
  • Introduces a rule-driven ICoT data-synthesis pipeline to generate structured scene descriptions, reasoning chains, and labels to reduce annotation costs.
  • Uses reinforcement learning with a composite reward to balance causal coherence and policy adherence, and a multitask architecture to detect intra-modal manipulations and cross-modal mismatches (e.g., subtitle-speech drift).
  • Shows improved accuracy, consistency, and generalization on real short-video ad moderation tasks compared to strong baselines.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Yiran Yang', 'Zhaowei Liu', 'Yuan Yuan', 'Yukun Song', 'Xiong Ma', 'Yinghao Song', 'Xiangji Zeng', 'Lu Sun', 'Yulu Wang', 'Hai Zhou', 'Shuai Cui', 'Zhaohan Gong', 'Jiefei Zhang']
  • Tags: ['content moderation', 'multimodal safety', 'explainability (chain-of-thought)', 'policy-aligned RL', 'robustness to cross-modal manipulation']

item.title: Expanding the Role of Diffusion Models for Robust Classifier Training
item.description:
Summary

  • Extends the use of diffusion models for adversarial robustness beyond synthetic data generation by leveraging their internal representations as auxiliary signals during adversarial training (AT).
  • Finds diffusion representations are diverse and partially robust; incorporating them into AT consistently improves classifier robustness and encourages more disentangled features.
  • Shows diffusion-generated data and diffusion representations play complementary roles in shaping robust representations.
  • Validates results with systematic experiments on CIFAR-10, CIFAR-100, and ImageNet.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Pin-Han Huang', 'Shang-Tse Chen', 'Hsuan-Tien Lin']
  • Tags: ['adversarial robustness', 'diffusion models', 'adversarial training', 'representation learning', 'synthetic data']

item.title: PrivacyBench: Privacy Isn't Free in Hybrid Privacy-Preserving Vision Systems
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces PrivacyBench, a benchmarking framework to evaluate interactions and trade-offs when composing privacy-preserving techniques in computer vision systems.
  • Finds severe non-additive failures: e.g., Federated Learning (FL) + Differential Privacy (DP) caused catastrophic convergence (accuracy drop from 98% to 13%) and large compute/energy overheads, while FL + SMPC retained near-baseline performance with modest cost.
  • Provides automated YAML configuration, resource monitoring, and reproducible protocols to help practitioners identify problematic technique combinations before deployment.

Paper Type

  • Benchmarking

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Nnaemeka Obiefuna', 'Samuel Oyeneye', 'Similoluwa Odunaiya', 'Iremide Oyelaja', 'Steven Kolawole']
  • Tags: ['privacy-preserving-ml', 'federated-learning', 'differential-privacy', 'secure-multiparty-computation', 'benchmarking']

item.title: Triggering hallucinations in model-based MRI reconstruction via adversarial perturbations
item.description:
Summary

  • Crafts adversarial perturbations (noise-like) to input MRI data that induce hallucinated features in reconstructions produced by generative models (UNet and end-to-end VarNet).
  • Evaluates attack effectiveness on brain and knee images from the fastMRI dataset, showing small perturbations reliably cause hallucinations that standard image-quality metrics fail to detect.
  • Discusses security implications for medical diagnosis, suggests adversarial training as a potential mitigation, and calls for novel detection methods for hallucinations.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Suna Bu\\u{g}day', 'Yvan Saeys', 'Jonathan Peck']
  • Tags: ['adversarial examples', 'medical imaging', 'model robustness', 'hallucinations', 'MRI reconstruction']

item.title: Benchmarking Unlearning for Vision Transformers
item.description:
Summary

  • First benchmark study of machine unlearning (MU) applied to Vision Transformers (ViT, Swin-T) across different model capacities.
  • Compares multiple MU algorithms (including methods that leverage training-data memorization) under single-shot and continual unlearning protocols on datasets of varying scale and complexity.
  • Introduces unified evaluation metrics capturing forget quality plus accuracy on unseen and retained data, analyzes how VTs memorize compared to CNNs, and establishes baseline MU performance for VTs.

Paper Type

  • Benchmarking

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Kairan Zhao', 'Iurie Luca', 'Peter Triantafillou']
  • Tags: ['machine unlearning', 'privacy', 'defense', 'benchmark', 'vision transformers']

item.title: The Invisible Gorilla Effect in Out-of-distribution Detection
item.description:
Summary

  • Identifies the "Invisible Gorilla Effect": OOD detection for near-OOD artefacts improves when artefact visual features (e.g., colour) match the model's ROI and degrades when they do not.
  • Empirical study: annotated 11,355 images across public vision datasets, created colour-swapped counterfactuals, and evaluated 40 OOD detection methods across 7 benchmarks.
  • Finds substantial performance drops for most methods when artefacts differ from ROI (e.g., Mahalanobis Score AUROC 31.5% higher for red vs. black ink in a skin-lesion classifier), exposing a systematic failure mode in OOD detectors.
  • Provides guidance for building more robust OOD detectors; artifacts, annotations, and code are released.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Harry Anthony', 'Ziyun Liang', 'Hermione Warr', 'Konstantinos Kamnitsas']
  • Tags: ['out-of-distribution_detection', 'robustness', 'failure_modes', 'vision']

item.title: TextShield-R1: Reinforced Reasoning for Tampered Text Detection
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes TextShield-R1, an RL-based multimodal LLM system for detecting and reasoning about tampered text in images.
  • Introduces Forensic Continual Pre-training (easy-to-hard curriculum) and Group Relative Policy Optimization with novel rewards to reduce annotation dependence and improve reasoning.
  • At inference, applies OCR Rectification to refine localization; releases TFR benchmark (45k+ images, 16 languages, 10 tampering techniques) for rigorous evaluation.
  • Reports significant SOTA gains in interpretable tampered-text detection and robust cross-style/method/language evaluation.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Chenfan Qu', 'Yiwu Zhong', 'Jian Liu', 'Xuekang Zhu', 'Bohan Yu', 'Lianwen Jin']
  • Tags: ['multimedia forensics', 'tampered-text detection', 'defense/detection', 'reinforcement learning', 'benchmark/dataset']

item.title: Pixels Don't Lie (But Your Detector Might): Bootstrapping MLLM-as-a-Judge for Trustworthy Deepfake Detection and Reasoning Supervision
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes DeepfakeJudge, an MLLM-as-a-judge framework for scalable reasoning supervision and evaluation of deepfake detectors.
  • Provides an out-of-distribution benchmark with recent generative/editing forgeries and a human-annotated subset with visual reasoning labels.
  • Introduces a bootstrapped generator–evaluator process that scales human feedback into structured reasoning supervision and achieves high correlation with human ratings and strong pairwise agreement.
  • Shows the reasoning-supervised judge outperforms much larger baselines in reasoning evaluation and yields reasonings preferred by users for faithfulness and groundedness.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Kartik Kuckreja', 'Parul Gupta', 'Muhammad Haris Khan', 'Abhinav Dhall']
  • Tags: ['deepfake-detection', 'evaluation-benchmark', 'MLLM-judge', 'reasoning-fidelity', 'scalable-supervision']

item.title: Localized Concept Erasure in Text-to-Image Diffusion Models via High-Level Representation Misdirection
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes HiRM (High-Level Representation Misdirection), a method that redirects high-level semantic representations in the text encoder to designated vectors to erase target concepts in text-to-image diffusion models.
  • Updates only early text-encoder layers (where visual-attribute causal states reside) to minimize degradation of non-target concepts and preserve generative quality.
  • Shows strong empirical results on UnlearnCanvas and NSFW benchmarks across diverse targets (objects, styles, nudity), transfers to other architectures, and complements denoiser-based concept-erasing methods.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Uichan Lee', 'Jeonghyeon Kim', 'Sangheum Hwang']
  • Tags: ['concept erasure', 'model editing', 'AI safety/defense', 'text-to-image diffusion', 'representation misdirection']

item.title: Learning Mutual View Information Graph for Adaptive Adversarial Collaborative Perception
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes MVIG attack, an adaptive adversarial framework that learns vulnerability information disclosed by defensive collaborative perception (CP) systems via a mutual view information graph.
  • Combines MVIG with temporal graph learning to produce evolving fabrication risk maps and uses an entropy-aware vulnerability search to optimize attack location, timing, and persistence.
  • Evaluated on OPV2V and Adv-OPV2V, showing large drops in defense success rates (up to 62%) and lower detection for persistent attacks, demonstrating practical security gaps in CP for connected/autonomous vehicles.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Yihang Tao', 'Senkang Hu', 'Haonan An', 'Zhengru Fang', 'Hangcheng Cao', 'Yuguang Fang']
  • Tags: ['adversarial attacks', 'collaborative perception', 'autonomous vehicles', 'graph-based attack', 'evasion']

item.title: VALD: Multi-Stage Vision Attack Detection for Efficient LVLM Defense
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes VALD, a multi-stage, training-free defense for LVLMs that detects and mitigates adversarial images via cheap image transformations, text-embedding discrepancy checks, and selective LLM invocation.
  • Uses a two-stage detection pipeline to quickly filter most clean inputs, invoking expensive resolution only for ambiguous cases and consolidating multiple model responses to recover correct outputs.
  • Demonstrates state-of-the-art accuracy with high efficiency: most clean images bypass costly processing and overall overhead remains low even with many adversarial examples.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Nadav Kadvil', 'Ayellet Tal']
  • Tags: ['adversarial examples', 'defense', 'detection', 'runtime mitigation']

item.title: Can a Teenager Fool an AI? Evaluating Low-Cost Cosmetic Attacks on Age Estimation Systems
item.description:
Summary

  • Evaluates low-cost, household-accessible cosmetic attacks (beards, grey hair, makeup, simulated wrinkles) simulated via a VLM image editor to see if minors can be misclassified as adults by age-estimation systems.
  • Tests eight models (five specialized age-estimators and three vision-language models) on 329 images of individuals aged 10–21 and introduces Attack Conversion Rate (ACR) as a population-agnostic metric.
  • Finds high vulnerability: synthetic beard yields 28–69% ACR; combined attacks shift predicted age by +7.7 years on average and reach up to 83% ACR; VLMs show somewhat lower ACR but overlap with specialized models.
  • Concludes that age-verification pipelines are critically vulnerable and recommends adversarial robustness evaluation as a mandatory criterion for model selection.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Xingyu Shen', 'Tommy Duong', 'Xiaodong An', 'Zengqi Zhao', 'Zebang Hu', 'Haoyu Hu', 'Ziyou Wang', 'Finn Guo', 'Simiao Ren']
  • Tags: ['adversarial attack', 'physical/cosmetic attacks', 'adversarial robustness', 'age estimation', 'vision-language models']

item.title: PA-Attack: Guiding Gray-Box Attacks on LVLM Vision Encoders with Prototypes and Attention
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces PA-Attack, a gray-box adversarial attack targeting LVLM vision encoders using prototype-anchored guidance to direct perturbations toward dissimilar prototypes for better generalization.
  • Proposes a two-stage attention enhancement: (i) focus perturbations on critical visual tokens via token-level attention scores, and (ii) adaptively recalibrate attention weights during iterative attacks.
  • Demonstrates strong cross-task and cross-model transferability on diverse LVLM architectures with an average 75.1% score reduction rate (SRR); code released publicly.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Hefei Mei', 'Zirui Wang', 'Chang Xu', 'Jianyuan Guo', 'Minjing Dong']
  • Tags: ['Adversarial examples', 'Gray-box attacks', 'Vision-Language Models', 'Attention-based attacks', 'Prototype-guided attacks']

item.title: UP-Fuse: Uncertainty-guided LiDAR-Camera Fusion for 3D Panoptic Segmentation
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes UP-Fuse, an uncertainty-guided LiDAR-camera fusion framework in the 2D range-view that modulates cross-modal interaction using predicted uncertainty maps to avoid corrupt visual cues.
  • Uncertainty maps are learned by quantifying representational divergence under diverse visual degradations, enabling robustness to camera degradation, calibration drift, and sensor failure.
  • Introduces a hybrid 2D-3D transformer decoder to mitigate spatial ambiguities from range-view projection and directly predict 3D panoptic segmentation masks.
  • Evaluated on Panoptic nuScenes, SemanticKITTI, and a new Panoptic Waymo benchmark, showing maintained performance under severe visual corruption and misalignment.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Rohit Mohan', 'Florian Drews', 'Yakov Miron', 'Daniele Cattaneo', 'Abhinav Valada']
  • Tags: ['robustness', 'sensor-fusion', 'uncertainty-estimation', 'perception-safety', 'calibration-robustness']

item.title: DefenseSplat: Enhancing the Robustness of 3D Gaussian Splatting via Frequency-Aware Filtering
item.description:
Summary

  • Identifies adversarial vulnerabilities in 3D Gaussian Splatting (3DGS) where imperceptible perturbations to input views degrade rendering quality, increase compute/memory, and can enable denial-of-service.
  • Analyzes adversarial perturbations via wavelet transforms to show distinct low- vs high-frequency behavior.
  • Proposes a frequency-aware defense that filters high-frequency noise while preserving low-frequency content to reconstruct training views, improving robustness without access to clean supervision.
  • Demonstrates substantial robustness gains across attack intensities and benchmarks with minimal impact on clean-data performance.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Yiran Qiao', 'Yiren Lu', 'Yunlai Zhou', 'Rui Yang', 'Linlin Hou', 'Yu Yin', 'Jing Ma']
  • Tags: ['adversarial-robustness', 'defense', '3D-Gaussian-Splatting', 'frequency-filtering', 'wavelet-analysis']

item.title: TokenTrace: Multi-Concept Attribution through Watermarked Token Recovery
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces TokenTrace, a proactive watermarking framework that embeds secret signatures into the semantic domain by perturbing text prompt embeddings and initial latent noise for diffusion models.
  • Presents a query-based retrieval module that, given a generated image and a textual query specifying a concept (e.g., object or style), disentangles and verifies multiple concept-specific watermarks from a single image.
  • Reports state-of-the-art results on single- and multi-concept attribution tasks, with high visual quality and robustness to common image transformations.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Li Zhang', 'Shruti Agarwal', 'John Collomosse', 'Pengtao Xie', 'Vishal Asnani']
  • Tags: ['watermarking', 'model attribution', 'IP protection', 'diffusion models', 'multimodal forensics']

item.title: Face Presentation Attack Detection via Content-Adaptive Spatial Operators
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes CASO-PAD, a lightweight RGB-only single-frame Face Presentation Attack Detection (FacePAD) model that augments MobileNetV3 with content-adaptive spatial operators (involution) to capture localized spoof cues.
  • Achieves strong results across multiple FacePAD benchmarks (Replay-Attack, Replay-Mobile, ROSE-Youtu, OULU-NPU, SiW-Mv2) with very low HTER/EER while remaining computationally efficient for on-device deployment.
  • Includes ablation studies on operator placement and group sharing to balance accuracy and efficiency, and trains end-to-end with a binary cross-entropy objective.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Shujaat Khan']
  • Tags: ['face-presentation-attack-detection', 'face-anti-spoofing', 'biometric-security', 'involution', 'mobile-friendly']

item.title: FOCA: Frequency-Oriented Cross-Domain Forgery Detection, Localization and Explanation via Multi-Modal Large Language Model
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes FOCA, a multimodal LLM-based framework that fuses RGB spatial and frequency-domain features via a cross-attention module for image forgery detection and pixel-level localization.
  • Provides explicit, human-interpretable cross-domain explanations linking spatial and textural (frequency) tampering cues.
  • Introduces FSE-Set, a large-scale dataset with authentic/tampered images, pixel masks, and dual-domain annotations to benchmark detection, localization, and explanation.
  • Demonstrates state-of-the-art detection/localization performance and improved interpretability over prior methods in extensive experiments.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Zhou Liu', 'Tonghua Su', 'Hongshi Zhang', 'Fuxiang Yang', 'Donglin Di', 'Yang Song', 'Lei Fan']
  • Tags: ['image forgery detection', 'digital forensics', 'frequency-domain analysis', 'explainable AI', 'multimodal models']

item.title: Echoes of Ownership: Adversarial-Guided Dual Injection for Copyright Protection in MLLMs
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes a method to generate trigger images that embed verifiable ownership information into multimodal LLMs (MLLMs) via adversarial-guided dual injection.
  • Dual injection: (1) enforce textual consistency between an auxiliary MLLM output and a target ownership text (backpropagating into the image), and (2) align CLIP image features with the target text; plus adversarial training to make triggers robust against heavily fine-tuned derivatives.
  • Aims to produce triggers that elicit ownership-related textual responses only in fine-tuned derivatives of the original model and remain inert in non-derivative models; evaluated across fine-tuning and domain-shift scenarios for tracking model lineage.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Chengwei Xia', 'Fan Ma', 'Ruijie Quan', 'Yunqiu Xu', 'Kun Zhan', 'Yi Yang']
  • Tags: ['model watermarking', 'copyright protection', 'adversarial techniques', 'ownership verification', 'multimodal models']

item.title: Detecting AI-Generated Forgeries via Iterative Manifold Deviation Amplification
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes IFA-Net (Iterative Forgery Amplifier Network) for pixel-level localization of AI-generated/manipulated images.
  • Leverages a frozen Masked Autoencoder (MAE) pretrained on real images as a universal realness prior; a Dual-Stream Segmentation Network fuses the input with MAE reconstruction residuals for coarse detection.
  • Introduces a Task-Adaptive Prior Injection (TAPI) module that turns coarse predictions into prompts to steer the MAE decoder and iteratively amplify reconstruction failures for precise refinement.
  • Reports strong gains on diffusion-based inpainting benchmarks (≈6.5% IoU, 8.1% F1) and good generalization to traditional manipulation types.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Jiangling Zhang', 'Shuxuan Gao', 'Bofan Liu', 'Siqiang Feng', 'Jirui Huang', 'Yaxiong Chen', 'Ziyu Chen']
  • Tags: ['image-forensics', 'deepfake-detection', 'forgery-localization', 'defense', 'masked-autoencoder']

item.title: HIME: Mitigating Object Hallucinations in LVLMs via Hallucination Insensitivity Model Editing
item.description:
Summary

  • Analyzes layer-wise susceptibility to object hallucination in LVLM decoders built on Qwen, LLaMA, and Vicuna backbones.
  • Proposes the Hallucination Insensitivity Score (HIS) to quantify each layer's sensitivity and guide targeted intervention.
  • Introduces HIME (Hallucination Insensitivity Model Editing), a layer-adaptive, training-free weight editing method that selectively modifies latent features to suppress hallucinations while preserving pre-trained knowledge.
  • Reports an average 61.8% reduction in hallucinations on open-ended benchmarks (CHAIR, MME, GPT-4V-aided evaluation) without extra parameters, latency, or computational overhead.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Ahmed Akl', 'Abdelwahed Khamis', 'Ali Cheraghian', 'Zhe Wang', 'Sara Khalifa', 'Kewen Wang']
  • Tags: ['hallucination mitigation', 'model editing', 'LVLM robustness', 'safety/defense', 'layer-wise analysis']

item.title: Suppression or Deletion: A Restoration-Based Representation-Level Analysis of Machine Unlearning
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes a restoration-based, representation-level evaluation using Sparse Autoencoders and inference-time steering to distinguish suppression from true deletion in machine unlearning.
  • Evaluates 12 major unlearning methods on image classification and finds most methods only suppress information at decision boundaries while semantic features remain recoverable in intermediate representations.
  • Shows even retraining from pretrained checkpoints often fails to remove semantic features inherited from pretraining, posing privacy risks and motivating new evaluation guidelines prioritizing representation-level verification.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Yurim Jang', 'Jaeung Lee', 'Dohyun Kim', 'Jaemin Jo', 'Simon S. Woo']
  • Tags: ['machine unlearning', 'privacy', 'representation-level vulnerability', 'defense evaluation']

item.title: Mitigating Shortcut Learning via Feature Disentanglement in Medical Imaging: A Benchmark Study
item.description:
Summary

  • Systematic evaluation of feature disentanglement methods (e.g., adversarial learning, latent-space dependence minimization) to mitigate shortcut learning from spurious correlations in medical imaging.
  • Benchmarks methods across one synthetic and two medical datasets with natural and synthetic confounders, assessing classification performance, disentanglement quality via latent-space analyses, robustness under varying confounding levels, and computational efficiency.
  • Finds that combining data-centric rebalancing with model-centric disentanglement yields stronger and more robust mitigation of shortcuts than rebalancing alone, while maintaining similar computational costs.
  • Latent-space analyses reveal representation differences not captured by classification metrics, highlighting strengths and limitations of individual methods.

Paper Type

  • Benchmarking

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Sarah M\\"uller', 'Philipp Berens']
  • Tags: ['shortcut-learning', 'feature-disentanglement', 'robustness', 'confounding', 'benchmark']

item.title: MCPShield: A Security Cognition Layer for Adaptive Trust Calibration in Model Context Protocol Agents
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes MCPShield, a plug-in security cognition layer for LLM agents that mitigates trust misalignment when invoking third-party Model Context Protocol (MCP) tools via metadata-guided probing, constrained execution, and runtime monitoring.
  • Incorporates post-invocation reasoning over historical traces to update agent security cognition, inspired by human pre-use validation and post-use reflection.
  • Evaluated on six novel MCP-based attack scenarios across six widely used agentic LLMs, reporting strong generalization, low false positives on benign servers, and low deployment overhead.
  • Aims to provide a practical defense mechanism for secure tool invocation in open agent ecosystems.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Zhenhong Zhou', 'Yuanhe Zhang', 'Hongwei Cai', 'Moayad Aloqaily', 'Ouns Bouachir', 'Linsey Pang', 'Prakhar Mehrotra', 'Kun Wang', 'Qingsong Wen']
  • Tags: ['agent security', 'tool invocation', 'protocol attacks', 'defense mechanism', 'runtime monitoring']

item.title: From Competition to Coordination: Market Making as a Scalable Framework for Safe and Aligned Multi-Agent LLM Systems
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes a market-making framework to coordinate multi-agent LLMs by having agents trade and update probabilistic beliefs, aligning local incentives with collective epistemic goals.
  • Claims improvements in factual reasoning, ethical judgment, and commonsense inference (up to ~10% accuracy gains) while preserving interpretability of intermediate reasoning.
  • Argues the economic coordination approach promotes self-organizing, verifiable, accountable, and more robust multi-agent behavior without centralized enforcement.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Brendan Gho', 'Suman Muppavarapu', 'Afnan Shaik', 'Tyson Tsay', 'Atharva Mohan', 'James Begin', 'Kevin Zhu', 'Archana Vaidheeswaran', 'Vasu Sharma']
  • Tags: ['multi-agent LLMs', 'alignment', 'coordination', 'robustness', 'market-making']

item.title: BEAT: Visual Backdoor Attacks on VLM-based Embodied Agents via Contrastive Trigger Learning
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces BEAT, a framework to inject visual backdoors into VLM-based embodied agents using objects in the environment as triggers that cause persistent attacker-specified multi-step policies.
  • Proposes a two-stage training pipeline: supervised fine-tuning (SFT) across diverse scenes/placements, followed by Contrastive Trigger Learning (CTL) which formulates trigger discrimination as preference learning to sharpen decision boundaries.
  • Demonstrates high attack success (up to 80%) while preserving benign performance, shows generalization to out-of-distribution trigger placements, and reports that CTL substantially outperforms naive SFT under limited backdoor data.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Qiusi Zhan', 'Hyeonjeong Ha', 'Rui Yang', 'Sirui Xu', 'Hanyang Chen', 'Liang-Yan Gui', 'Yu-Xiong Wang', 'Huan Zhang', 'Heng Ji', 'Daniel Kang']
  • Tags: ['visual backdoor', 'VLM security', 'embodied agent attacks', 'contrastive learning', 'adversarial attack']

item.title: SocialHarmBench: Revealing LLM Vulnerabilities to Socially Harmful Requests
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces SocialHarmBench, a 585-prompt benchmark across 7 sociopolitical categories and 34 countries to test LLM behavior on politically charged, socially harmful requests.
  • Evaluates multiple LLMs and finds high vulnerability to harmful compliance (e.g., open-weight models like Mistral-7B showing 97–98% attack success in domains such as propaganda, historical revisionism, and political manipulation).
  • Performs temporal and geographic analyses showing models are especially fragile on 21st-century/pre-20th-century contexts and regions such as Latin America, USA, and UK, highlighting failures of current safeguards.
  • Provides the dataset publicly (Hugging Face) to support red-teaming, safety evaluation, and mitigation research in sociopolitical harm domains.

Paper Type

  • Benchmarking

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Punya Syon Pandey', 'Hai Son Le', 'Devansh Bhardwaj', 'Rada Mihalcea', 'Zhijing Jin']
  • Tags: ['safety-benchmark', 'red-teaming', 'political-manipulation', 'dataset']

item.title: Role-Aware Language Models for Secure and Contextualized Access Control in Organizations
item.description:
Summary

  • Investigates fine-tuning LLMs to enforce role-specific access constraints in enterprise settings (role-conditioned generation and classifiers).
  • Proposes and compares three modeling strategies: BERT-based classifier, LLM-based classifier, and role-conditioned generation.
  • Constructs two datasets (adapted/clustered instruction-tuning corpus with role labels, and a synthetic enterprise role-sensitive dataset) for evaluation.
  • Evaluates robustness to security threats including prompt injection, role mismatch, and jailbreak attempts.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Saeed Almheiri', 'Yerulan Kongrat', 'Adrian Santosh', 'Ruslan Tasmukhanov', 'Josemaria Loza Vera', 'Muhammad Dehan Al Kautsar', 'Fajri Koto']
  • Tags: ['access control', 'prompt injection', 'jailbreak mitigation', 'role-based security', 'model robustness']

item.title: Accidental Vulnerability: Factors in Fine-Tuning that Shift Model Safeguards
item.description:
Summary

  • Investigates 'Accidental Vulnerability'—how characteristics of fine-tuning datasets (linguistic features, semantic similarity, toxicity) can introduce or amplify adversarial vulnerabilities in LLMs.
  • Evaluates adversarial robustness of fine-tuned models, analyzing persona shifts and interpretability to link dataset factors with attack success rates.
  • Explores causal relationships to inform defensive strategies and emphasizes dataset design as a critical component of preserving model alignment.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Punya Syon Pandey', 'Samuel Simko', 'Kellin Pelrine', 'Zhijing Jin']
  • Tags: ['adversarial vulnerability', 'fine-tuning', 'dataset design', 'robustness', 'causal analysis']

item.title: MANATEE: Inference-Time Lightweight Diffusion Based Safety Defense for LLMs
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes MANATEE, an inference-time defense that estimates the density/score function of benign hidden states and uses diffusion to project anomalous representations toward safe regions.
  • Targets adversarial jailbreak attacks on LLMs without requiring harmful training data, model fine-tuning, or architectural changes.
  • Demonstrates substantial reductions in attack success rate (up to 100% on some datasets) across multiple LLMs (Mistral-7B-Instruct, Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct, Gemma-2-9B-it) while preserving utility on benign inputs.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Chun Yan Ryan Kan', 'Tommy Tran', 'Vedant Yadav', 'Ava Cai', 'Kevin Zhu', 'Ruizhe Li', 'Maheep Chaudhary']
  • Tags: ['adversarial-defense', 'jailbreak-mitigation', 'inference-time-defense', 'diffusion-based-defense']

item.title: Watermarking LLM Agent Trajectories
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces ActHook, a watermarking method that embeds hook actions into LLM agent trajectories triggered by a secret key, preserving original task outcomes.
  • Hook actions are inserted at decision points so that models trained on watermarked data will reproduce hooks at higher rates when given the key, enabling black-box detection of unauthorized use.
  • Evaluated on mathematical reasoning, web searching, and software engineering agents, achieving an average detection AUC of 94.3 on Qwen-2.5-Coder-7B with negligible performance degradation.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Wenlong Meng', 'Chen Gong', 'Terry Yue Zhuo', 'Fan Zhang', 'Kecen Li', 'Zheng Liu', 'Zhou Yang', 'Chengkun Wei', 'Wenzhi Chen']
  • Tags: ['watermarking', 'dataset-provenance', 'model-ownership', 'LLM-agents', 'data-protection']

item.title: Spilled Energy in Large Language Models
item.description:
Summary

  • Reinterprets the LLM final softmax as an Energy-Based Model (EBM) and decomposes sequence decoding into interacting EBMs to track 'energy spills' across generation steps.
  • Introduces two training-free metrics from output logits — spilled energy (discrepancy across steps) and marginalized energy (single-step measure) — to localize answer tokens and detect hallucinations.
  • Empirically shows these metrics correlate with factual errors, biases, and failures and achieve robust hallucination-detection performance across nine benchmarks and multiple state-of-the-art LLMs (pretrained and instruction-tuned).
  • Does not require trained probes or activation ablations, offering a lightweight, generalizable safety/robustness tool for detecting hallucinations.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Adrian Robert Minut', 'Hazem Dewidar', 'Iacopo Masi']
  • Tags: ['hallucination_detection', 'energy-based_models', 'model_robustness', 'interpretability', 'safety_defense']

item.title: Vibe Coding on Trial: Operating Characteristics of Unanimous LLM Juries
item.description:
Summary

  • Benchmarks 15 open LLMs on 82 Text-to-SQL (MySQL) tasks using execution-grounded evaluation to identify strong models.
  • Constructs unanimous LLM committees (1–6 members drawn from top six models) that accept a candidate SQL only if every juror deems it correct, prioritizing minimizing false accepts.
  • Evaluates committees using true positive rate, false positive rate, and Youden J, showing small unanimous committees can substantially reduce false accepts while retaining many correct queries.
  • Finds committee composition materially affects performance, suggesting ensemble selection is important for safety-first deployment of automated code generation review.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Muhammad Aziz Ullah', 'Abdul Serwadda']
  • Tags: ['LLM-based defenses', 'Guardrails', 'Model ensembles / juries', 'Code generation safety', 'Benchmarking']

item.title: Red Teaming LLMs as Socio-Technical Practice: From Exploration and Data Creation to Evaluation
item.description:
Summary

  • Empirical qualitative study (22 practitioner interviews) of how red teaming datasets for LLMs are conceptualized, created, and evaluated as socio-technical artifacts.
  • Finds that current practitioner practices emphasize technical measures and adversarial datasets but often overlook contextual factors such as interaction type and user specificity when assessing risk.
  • Offers three opportunities for HCI researchers to broaden data practices and the conceptualization of red teaming to better capture real-world harms and evaluation scope.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Adriana Alvarado Garcia', 'Ruyuan Wan', 'Ozioma C. Oguine', 'Karla Badillo-Urquiola']
  • Tags: ['red-teaming', 'safety-evaluation', 'dataset-creation', 'socio-technical-practices', 'HCI-empirical-study']

item.title: AgenticSum: An Agentic Inference-Time Framework for Faithful Clinical Text Summarization
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes AgenticSum, an inference-time, multi-stage framework (context selection, generation, verification, targeted correction) to reduce hallucinations in clinical text summarization by LLMs.
  • Uses internal attention grounding signals to identify weakly supported spans and selectively revises flagged content under supervisory control.
  • Evaluates on two public clinical summarization datasets with reference metrics, LLM-as-judge assessments, and human evaluation, showing consistent improvements over vanilla LLMs and strong baselines.
  • Focuses on an agentic, structured defense at inference time to improve factual consistency and reduce hallucinated content in clinical notes.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Fahmida Liza Piya', 'Rahmatollah Beheshti']
  • Tags: ['hallucination_mitigation', 'factuality', 'inference-time_defense', 'clinical_NLP', 'LLM_verification_and_revision']

item.title: Assessing Risks of Large Language Models in Mental Health Support: A Framework for Automated Clinical AI Red Teaming
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes an automated clinical red teaming framework pairing LLM-based AI psychotherapists with simulated patient agents using dynamic cognitive-affective models to evaluate longitudinal therapy interactions.
  • Applies the framework to Alcohol Use Disorder across 15 clinically-validated patient personas and 369 simulated sessions evaluating six AI agents (including ChatGPT, Gemini, Character.AI).
  • Finds critical safety vulnerabilities (e.g., validation of delusions, failure to de-escalate suicide risk), and provides a stakeholder-validated interactive dashboard to audit model behavior and safety failure modes.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Ian Steenstra', 'Paola Pedrelli', 'Weiyan Shi', 'Stacy Marsella', 'Timothy W. Bickmore']
  • Tags: ['red teaming', 'safety evaluation', 'LLM vulnerabilities', 'clinical AI', 'simulation-based testing']

item.title: Anatomy of Unlearning: The Dual Impact of Fact Salience and Model Fine-Tuning
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces DUAL, a 28.6k Wikidata-derived benchmark annotated with fact popularity (Wikipedia link counts) and LLM-based salience scores to evaluate machine unlearning.
  • Compares unlearning behavior across training stages, showing supervised fine-tuning (SFT) on forget data produces smoother forgetting, more stable tuning, and 10–50% higher retention.
  • Finds direct unlearning on pretrained models is unstable and prone to relearning or catastrophic forgetting, highlighting the dual impact of fact salience and training-stage provenance on unlearning effectiveness.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Borisiuk Anna', 'Andrey Savchenko', 'Alexander Panchecko', 'Elena Tutubalina']
  • Tags: ['machine-unlearning', 'model-editing', 'privacy', 'benchmark', 'LLM-safety']

item.title: Luna-2: Scalable Single-Token Evaluation with Small Language Models
item.description:
Summary

  • Presents Luna-2, an architecture that uses decoder-only small language models with lightweight LoRA/PEFT heads to perform deterministic, single-token evaluation for safety metrics (toxicity, hallucination, tool selection, etc.).
  • Targets real-time guardrails by providing accurate, cheap, and low-latency evaluation comparable to LLM-as-a-judge using frontier LLMs while reducing inference cost and latency substantially.
  • Enables hundreds of specialized metrics to run concurrently on a single GPU, suitable for local, privacy-preserving deployment; reports production-scale throughput and large cost savings.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Vatsal Goel', 'Rishon Dsouza', 'Nikhil Ega', 'Amey Ramesh Rambatla', 'Rob Friel', 'Shuai Shao', 'Yash Sheth']
  • Tags: ['safety/guardrails', 'evaluation/LLM-as-judge', 'hallucination detection', 'toxicity detection', 'deployable defenses']

item.title: High-Dimensional Asymptotics of Differentially Private PCA
item.description:
Summary

  • Analyzes the privacy loss of the exponential mechanism for differentially private PCA in the high-dimensional limit (p → ∞), providing sharp, dataset-dependent characterizations.
  • Shows that detecting an individual's presence from privatized principal components is asymptotically equivalent to distinguishing between two Gaussians whose mean separation depends on spectral properties of the data.
  • Combines hypothesis-testing formulation of privacy (Dong, Roth, Su) with Le Cam contiguity arguments to obtain tight privacy and utility tradeoffs in a model-free setting.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Youngjoo Yun', 'Rishabh Dudeja']
  • Tags: ['differential privacy', 'privacy analysis', 'PCA', 'high-dimensional asymptotics', 'hypothesis testing']

item.title: Controllable Collision Scenario Generation via Collision Pattern Prediction
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces controllable collision scenario generation for autonomous vehicles: generate adversarial trajectories realizing user-specified collision type and time-to-accident (TTA).
  • Presents COLLIDE, a large-scale dataset of synthesized collisions balanced across collision types and TTA intervals.
  • Proposes a framework that predicts a compact, interpretable Collision Pattern (spatial configuration at impact) before rolling out full adversarial trajectories, improving collision rate and controllability over baselines.
  • Uses generated scenarios to evaluate planner failures and fine-tune planners to improve robustness across collision scenarios (adversarial evaluation / robustness testing).

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Pin-Lun Chen', 'Chi-Hsi Kung', 'Che-Han Chang', 'Wei-Chen Chiu', 'Yi-Ting Chen']
  • Tags: ['adversarial evaluation', 'autonomous vehicles', 'scenario generation', 'robustness', 'dataset']

item.title: SocialHarmBench: Revealing LLM Vulnerabilities to Socially Harmful Requests
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces SocialHarmBench, a 585-prompt benchmark across 7 sociopolitical categories and 34 countries to test LLM behavior on politically charged, socially harmful requests.
  • Evaluates multiple LLMs and finds high vulnerability to harmful compliance (e.g., open-weight models like Mistral-7B showing 97–98% attack success in domains such as propaganda, historical revisionism, and political manipulation).
  • Performs temporal and geographic analyses showing models are especially fragile on 21st-century/pre-20th-century contexts and regions such as Latin America, USA, and UK, highlighting failures of current safeguards.
  • Provides the dataset publicly (Hugging Face) to support red-teaming, safety evaluation, and mitigation research in sociopolitical harm domains.

Paper Type

  • Benchmarking

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Punya Syon Pandey', 'Hai Son Le', 'Devansh Bhardwaj', 'Rada Mihalcea', 'Zhijing Jin']
  • Tags: ['safety-benchmark', 'red-teaming', 'political-manipulation', 'dataset']

item.title: Accidental Vulnerability: Factors in Fine-Tuning that Shift Model Safeguards
item.description:
Summary

  • Investigates 'Accidental Vulnerability'—how characteristics of fine-tuning datasets (linguistic features, semantic similarity, toxicity) can introduce or amplify adversarial vulnerabilities in LLMs.
  • Evaluates adversarial robustness of fine-tuned models, analyzing persona shifts and interpretability to link dataset factors with attack success rates.
  • Explores causal relationships to inform defensive strategies and emphasizes dataset design as a critical component of preserving model alignment.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Punya Syon Pandey', 'Samuel Simko', 'Kellin Pelrine', 'Zhijing Jin']
  • Tags: ['adversarial vulnerability', 'fine-tuning', 'dataset design', 'robustness', 'causal analysis']

item.title: Conformal Signal Temporal Logic for Robust Reinforcement Learning Control: A Case Study
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes a runtime safety mechanism — a conformal Signal Temporal Logic (STL) shield — that filters actions from a PPO reinforcement learning agent using online conformal prediction to enforce temporal logic specifications.
  • Implements the method in the AeroBench F-16 simulation: task is to regulate engine throttle and maintain airspeed within an STL-specified band during maneuver end phases.
  • Compares three settings (PPO baseline, PPO + classical rule-based STL shield, PPO + conformal STL shield) under nominal and severe stress scenarios (model mismatch, actuator limits, noise, setpoint jumps).
  • Reports that the conformal shield preserves STL satisfaction while maintaining near-baseline performance and yields stronger robustness guarantees than the classical shield.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Hani Beirami', 'M M Manjurul Islam']
  • Tags: ['runtime shielding', 'conformal prediction', 'Signal Temporal Logic (STL)', 'robust reinforcement learning', 'safety/guardrails']

item.title: SAGE-5GC: Security-Aware Guidelines for Evaluating Anomaly Detection in the 5G Core Network
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes SAGE-5GC: security-aware guidelines for evaluating ML-based anomaly detectors in 5G Core networks under realistic (non-IID, adversarial) conditions.
  • Trains and assesses multiple anomaly detectors on a realistic 5G Core dataset against PFCP-based control-plane cyberattacks to establish baseline performance.
  • Analyzes adversarial robustness by identifying attacker-controllable features, performing randomized perturbation sensitivity analysis, and showing vulnerabilities.
  • Implements a practical black-box genetic-algorithm attack that manipulates only controllable features while preserving malicious functionality, demonstrating substantial evasion success.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Cristian Manca', 'Christian Scano', 'Giorgio Piras', 'Fabio Brau', 'Maura Pintor', 'Battista Biggio']
  • Tags: ['adversarial evasion', 'anomaly detection', '5G network security', 'robustness evaluation', 'black-box attacks']

item.title: Improving the Trade-off Between Watermark Strength and Speculative Sampling Efficiency for Language Models
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces a quantitative measure of watermark strength that governs statistical detectability and is maximized when tokens are deterministic functions of pseudorandom numbers.
  • Frames the trade-off between watermark strength and speculative sampling acceptance as a constrained optimization problem and derives explicit Pareto curves for two existing watermarking schemes.
  • Proposes a mechanism that injects pseudorandomness into draft-token acceptance to achieve maximal watermark strength while preserving speculative sampling efficiency, with experiments showing improved detectability without loss of efficiency.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Weiqing He', 'Xiang Li', 'Li Shen', 'Weijie Su', 'Qi Long']
  • Tags: ['watermarking', 'LLM detection', 'speculative sampling', 'inference efficiency', 'model provenance']

item.title: Explainability Methods for Hardware Trojan Detection: A Systematic Comparison
item.description:
Summary

  • Systematic comparison of three explainability approaches for gate-level hardware Trojan detection on the Trust-Hub benchmark: domain-aware property analysis, case-based reasoning (k-NN), and model-agnostic feature attribution (LIME, SHAP, gradients).
  • Evaluates 31 circuit-specific features derived from gate fan-in patterns, flip-flop distances, and I/O connectivity to provide actionable, domain-aware explanations for hardware engineers.
  • Assesses whether general-purpose explainability methods offer useful insights for hardware security and contrasts them with domain-informed techniques to reduce false positives/negatives in Trojan detection.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Paul Whitten', 'Francis Wolff', 'Chris Papachristou']
  • Tags: ['hardware-security', 'hardware-trojans', 'explainability', 'attack-detection', 'benchmarking']

item.title: Inverting Self-Organizing Maps: A Unified Activation-Based Framework
item.description:
Summary

  • Shows that a SOM's activation pattern (squared distances to prototypes) can be inverted to recover the exact input when distances to D+1 affinely independent prototypes are available; derives the linear system and conditions for well-posed inversion.
  • Introduces MUSIC, an update rule that modifies squared distances to selected prototypes while preserving others to produce controlled, semantically meaningful trajectories in input space without learned decoders or latent priors.
  • Uses Tikhonov regularization for stability in high dimensions and validates on synthetic Gaussian mixtures, MNIST, and LFW, demonstrating sharp intermediate images and high classifier confidence along trajectories.
  • Although framed as a generative/control method, the work effectively demonstrates a model-inversion-style reconstruction capability from SOM activations, which has privacy/security implications.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Alessandro Londei', 'Matteo Benati', 'Denise Lanzieri', 'Vittorio Loreto']
  • Tags: ['model inversion', 'privacy', 'self-organizing maps', 'generative control', 'reconstruction']

item.title: Leak@$k$: Unlearning Does Not Make LLMs Forget Under Probabilistic Decoding
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces leak@k, a meta-evaluation metric that measures the probability that 'forgotten' information reappears when drawing k samples under probabilistic decoding.
  • Performs a large-scale empirical study on TOFU, MUSE, and WMDP showing that existing unlearning methods often fail: sensitive information resurfaces under standard sampling even when deterministic evaluations suggest successful forgetting.
  • Proposes RULE (Robust Unlearning under LEak@k) as an initial defense, demonstrating strong reduction of leakage on the TOFU benchmark across many sampled generations.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Hadi Reisizadeh', 'Jiajun Ruan', 'Yiwei Chen', 'Soumyadeep Pal', 'Sijia Liu', 'Mingyi Hong']
  • Tags: ['unlearning', 'privacy/memorization', 'evaluation-metric', 'defense', 'model robustness']

item.title: Towards Privacy-Aware Bayesian Networks: A Credal Approach
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes using credal networks (CNs) to mask learned Bayesian networks (BNs) as a privacy-preserving alternative to parameter-noising techniques.
  • Adapts the notion of tracing attacks (membership/traceability attacks) against BNs and demonstrates that CNs reduce the probability of successful attacks while retaining useful inference capabilities.
  • Identifies which aspects of BN learning must be concealed to prevent recovery by attackers and empirically analyzes the privacy–utility tradeoff by tuning CN hyperparameters.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Niccol\\`o Rocchi', 'Fabio Stella', 'Cassio de Campos']
  • Tags: ['privacy-preserving ML', 'membership inference / tracing attacks', 'probabilistic graphical models', 'defense', 'credal networks']

item.title: Sampling-aware Adversarial Attacks Against Large Language Models
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces sampling-aware adversarial attacks that exploit stochastic sampling of LLM outputs in addition to prompt optimization to elicit harmful responses.
  • Frames attacks as a resource allocation problem between optimization and output sampling, empirically identifying compute-optimal trade-offs and showing large gains in success rate and efficiency.
  • Analyzes how output-harmfulness distributions evolve during attacks and proposes a label-free entropy-maximization objective enabled by the sampling-aware perspective.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Tim Beyer', 'Yan Scholten', 'Leo Schwinn', 'Stephan G\\"unnemann']
  • Tags: ['adversarial-attacks', 'robustness', 'sampling-based-attacks', 'safety-evaluation']

item.title: GRILL: Restoring Gradient Signal in Ill-Conditioned Layers for More Effective Adversarial Attacks on Autoencoders
item.description:
Summary

  • Identifies a failure mode in white-box adversarial attacks on autoencoders caused by vanishing adversarial-loss gradients through ill-conditioned layers (near-zero Jacobian singular values).
  • Proposes GRILL, a method to locally restore gradient signals in ill-conditioned layers to enable stronger norm-bounded (sample-specific and universal) attacks.
  • Demonstrates across multiple AE architectures and adaptive attack settings that GRILL substantially increases attack effectiveness and reveals greater vulnerability in AE robustness evaluations.
  • Provides empirical evidence that encoder–decoder components in modern multimodal architectures exhibit similar vulnerabilities exploitable by GRILL.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Chethan Krishnamurthy Ramanaik', 'Arjun Roy', 'Tobias Callies', 'Eirini Ntoutsi']
  • Tags: ['adversarial attacks', 'autoencoders', 'gradient manipulation', 'robustness evaluation', 'multimodal vulnerabilities']

item.title: Skill-Inject: Measuring Agent Vulnerability to Skill File Attacks
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces SkillInject, a 202 attack-task benchmark measuring LLM agent vulnerability to skill-file prompt injection.
  • Evaluates frontier LLM agents on both security (harmful-instruction avoidance) and utility (legitimate instruction compliance), finding up to 80% attack success.
  • Demonstrates attacks enable data exfiltration, destructive actions, ransomware-like behavior, and argues scaling or simple input filtering are insufficient.
  • Releases benchmark and argues for context-aware authorization frameworks as part of robust agent security.

Paper Type

  • Benchmarking

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['David Schmotz', 'Luca Beurer-Kellner', 'Sahar Abdelnabi', 'Maksym Andriushchenko']
  • Tags: ['prompt injection', 'agent security', 'benchmark', 'adversarial attacks', 'data exfiltration']

item.title: The Invisible Gorilla Effect in Out-of-distribution Detection
item.description:
Summary

  • Identifies the "Invisible Gorilla Effect": OOD detection for near-OOD artefacts improves when artefact visual features (e.g., colour) match the model's ROI and degrades when they do not.
  • Empirical study: annotated 11,355 images across public vision datasets, created colour-swapped counterfactuals, and evaluated 40 OOD detection methods across 7 benchmarks.
  • Finds substantial performance drops for most methods when artefacts differ from ROI (e.g., Mahalanobis Score AUROC 31.5% higher for red vs. black ink in a skin-lesion classifier), exposing a systematic failure mode in OOD detectors.
  • Provides guidance for building more robust OOD detectors; artifacts, annotations, and code are released.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Harry Anthony', 'Ziyun Liang', 'Hermione Warr', 'Konstantinos Kamnitsas']
  • Tags: ['out-of-distribution_detection', 'robustness', 'failure_modes', 'vision']

item.title: Latent Introspection: Models Can Detect Prior Concept Injections
item.description:
Summary

  • Shows Qwen-32B has latent ability to detect when concepts were injected into its prior context via signals in the residual stream (logit lens), even if final outputs deny injection.
  • Demonstrates that prompting with information about introspection mechanisms greatly amplifies detection sensitivity (0.3% -> 39.2%) with minimal extra false positives and increases mutual information between injected/recovered concepts.
  • Finds detection signals are present in earlier layers but attenuated in final layers, with implications for latent reasoning, steering, and safety.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Theia Pearson-Vogel', 'Martin Vanek', 'Raymond Douglas', 'Jan Kulveit']
  • Tags: ['model introspection', 'prompt injection', 'logit lens / interpretability', 'safety / defenses', 'latent steering']

item.title: RobPI: Robust Private Inference against Malicious Client
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces an inference-manipulation attack by malicious clients that breaks state-of-the-art private inference protocols with 3x–8x fewer queries than prior black-box attacks.
  • Proposes RobPI, a private inference protocol that defends against malicious clients by injecting encryption-compatible noise into logits and features via a novel cryptographic workflow.
  • Empirical evaluation on multiple neural networks and datasets shows RobPI reduces attack success rate by ~91.9% and increases required attacker queries by over 10x.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Jiaqi Xue', 'Mengxin Zheng', 'Qian Lou']
  • Tags: ['private-inference', 'malicious-client', 'adversarial-attack', 'cryptographic-defense', 'robustness']

item.title: Dirichlet Scale Mixture Priors for Bayesian Neural Networks
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes Dirichlet scale mixture (DSM) priors for Bayesian neural networks to induce structured, sparsity-promoting shrinkage and implicit feature selection.
  • Provides theoretical analysis of dependence structures and shrinkage behavior of DSM priors in the geometry of neural networks.
  • Empirically shows DSM priors yield competitive predictive performance with fewer effective parameters, improved amenability to pruning, and robustness under adversarial attacks; also addresses cold-posterior issues via heavy-tailed shrinkage.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['August Arnstad', 'Leiv R{\\o}nneberg', 'Geir Storvik']
  • Tags: ['adversarial robustness', 'Bayesian neural networks', 'priors / shrinkage', 'model sparsity / pruning']

item.title: Can a Teenager Fool an AI? Evaluating Low-Cost Cosmetic Attacks on Age Estimation Systems
item.description:
Summary

  • Evaluates low-cost, household-accessible cosmetic attacks (beards, grey hair, makeup, simulated wrinkles) simulated via a VLM image editor to see if minors can be misclassified as adults by age-estimation systems.
  • Tests eight models (five specialized age-estimators and three vision-language models) on 329 images of individuals aged 10–21 and introduces Attack Conversion Rate (ACR) as a population-agnostic metric.
  • Finds high vulnerability: synthetic beard yields 28–69% ACR; combined attacks shift predicted age by +7.7 years on average and reach up to 83% ACR; VLMs show somewhat lower ACR but overlap with specialized models.
  • Concludes that age-verification pipelines are critically vulnerable and recommends adversarial robustness evaluation as a mandatory criterion for model selection.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Xingyu Shen', 'Tommy Duong', 'Xiaodong An', 'Zengqi Zhao', 'Zebang Hu', 'Haoyu Hu', 'Ziyou Wang', 'Finn Guo', 'Simiao Ren']
  • Tags: ['adversarial attack', 'physical/cosmetic attacks', 'adversarial robustness', 'age estimation', 'vision-language models']

item.title: IR$^3$: Contrastive Inverse Reinforcement Learning for Interpretable Detection and Mitigation of Reward Hacking
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces IR3, a framework using Contrastive Inverse Reinforcement Learning (C-IRL) to reconstruct implicit reward functions driving RLHF-tuned models by contrasting post-alignment and baseline policy outputs.
  • Decomposes reconstructed rewards with sparse autoencoders to produce interpretable features and uses contribution analysis to identify reward-hacking signatures.
  • Proposes mitigation techniques (clean reward optimization, adversarial shaping, constrained optimization, feature-guided distillation) and reports empirical results: 0.89 correlation with ground-truth rewards, >90% precision in detecting hacking features, and substantial reduction in hacking while retaining model capabilities within 3%.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Mohammad Beigi', 'Ming Jin', 'Junshan Zhang', 'Jiaxin Zhang', 'Qifan Wang', 'Lifu Huang']
  • Tags: ['reward-hacking', 'inverse-reinforcement-learning', 'RLHF', 'defenses', 'attack-detection']

item.title: Detecting Cybersecurity Threats by Integrating Explainable AI with SHAP Interpretability and Strategic Data Sampling
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes an integrated Explainable AI framework for cybersecurity threat detection combining strategic data sampling, automated data leakage prevention, and model-agnostic SHAP interpretability.
  • Aims to preserve class distributions and reduce computational overhead while maintaining detection performance (evaluated on the CIC-IDS2017 dataset).
  • Provides actionable explanations for security analysts to improve operational transparency in Security Operations Centers (SOCs).

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Norrakith Srisumrith', 'Sunantha Sodsee']
  • Tags: ['explainable-ai', 'cybersecurity-threat-detection', 'shap-interpretability', 'data-leakage-prevention', 'strategic-sampling']

item.title: Limits of Convergence-Rate Control for Open-Weight Safety
item.description:
Summary

  • Addresses training-resistance for open-weight foundation models by framing interventions as convergence-rate control problems tied to the spectral structure of model weights.
  • Proposes SpecDef, a spectral reparameterization algorithm that provably and empirically slows first- and second-order optimization in non-adversarial settings.
  • Proves a fundamental limitation: in adversarial settings an attacker with sufficient knowledge can restore fast convergence by increasing model size linearly, implying convergence-rate control methods have inherent limits.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Domenic Rosati', 'Xijie Zeng', 'Hong Huang', 'Sebastian Dionicio', 'Subhabrata Majumdar', 'Frank Rudzicz', 'Hassan Sajjad']
  • Tags: ['training-resistance', 'optimization-security', 'defenses', 'spectral-methods', 'adversarial-circumvention']

item.title: Operational Robustness of LLMs on Code Generation
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes a robustness evaluation method (scenario domain analysis) to find minimal changes in natural-language coding task descriptions that cause LLMs to produce incorrect code.
  • Provides theoretical analysis of the method and empirical evaluation across four code-generation LLMs (Gemini-pro, Codex, Llama 2, Falcon 7B), ranking their operational robustness.
  • Analyzes how robustness varies by task topic and solution complexity, finding lower robustness for more complex tasks and advanced topics (e.g., multi-threading, data structures).

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Debalina Ghosh Paul', 'Hong Zhu', 'Ian Bayley']
  • Tags: ['adversarial-robustness', 'robustness-evaluation', 'code-generation', 'adversarial-examples']

item.title: MANATEE: Inference-Time Lightweight Diffusion Based Safety Defense for LLMs
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes MANATEE, an inference-time defense that estimates the density/score function of benign hidden states and uses diffusion to project anomalous representations toward safe regions.
  • Targets adversarial jailbreak attacks on LLMs without requiring harmful training data, model fine-tuning, or architectural changes.
  • Demonstrates substantial reductions in attack success rate (up to 100% on some datasets) across multiple LLMs (Mistral-7B-Instruct, Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct, Gemma-2-9B-it) while preserving utility on benign inputs.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Chun Yan Ryan Kan', 'Tommy Tran', 'Vedant Yadav', 'Ava Cai', 'Kevin Zhu', 'Ruizhe Li', 'Maheep Chaudhary']
  • Tags: ['adversarial-defense', 'jailbreak-mitigation', 'inference-time-defense', 'diffusion-based-defense']

item.title: Luna-2: Scalable Single-Token Evaluation with Small Language Models
item.description:
Summary

  • Presents Luna-2, an architecture that uses decoder-only small language models with lightweight LoRA/PEFT heads to perform deterministic, single-token evaluation for safety metrics (toxicity, hallucination, tool selection, etc.).
  • Targets real-time guardrails by providing accurate, cheap, and low-latency evaluation comparable to LLM-as-a-judge using frontier LLMs while reducing inference cost and latency substantially.
  • Enables hundreds of specialized metrics to run concurrently on a single GPU, suitable for local, privacy-preserving deployment; reports production-scale throughput and large cost savings.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Vatsal Goel', 'Rishon Dsouza', 'Nikhil Ega', 'Amey Ramesh Rambatla', 'Rob Friel', 'Shuai Shao', 'Yash Sheth']
  • Tags: ['safety/guardrails', 'evaluation/LLM-as-judge', 'hallucination detection', 'toxicity detection', 'deployable defenses']

item.title: Triggering hallucinations in model-based MRI reconstruction via adversarial perturbations
item.description:
Summary

  • Crafts adversarial perturbations (noise-like) to input MRI data that induce hallucinated features in reconstructions produced by generative models (UNet and end-to-end VarNet).
  • Evaluates attack effectiveness on brain and knee images from the fastMRI dataset, showing small perturbations reliably cause hallucinations that standard image-quality metrics fail to detect.
  • Discusses security implications for medical diagnosis, suggests adversarial training as a potential mitigation, and calls for novel detection methods for hallucinations.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Suna Bu\\u{g}day', 'Yvan Saeys', 'Jonathan Peck']
  • Tags: ['adversarial examples', 'medical imaging', 'model robustness', 'hallucinations', 'MRI reconstruction']

item.title: Mitigating Shortcut Learning via Feature Disentanglement in Medical Imaging: A Benchmark Study
item.description:
Summary

  • Systematic evaluation of feature disentanglement methods (e.g., adversarial learning, latent-space dependence minimization) to mitigate shortcut learning from spurious correlations in medical imaging.
  • Benchmarks methods across one synthetic and two medical datasets with natural and synthetic confounders, assessing classification performance, disentanglement quality via latent-space analyses, robustness under varying confounding levels, and computational efficiency.
  • Finds that combining data-centric rebalancing with model-centric disentanglement yields stronger and more robust mitigation of shortcuts than rebalancing alone, while maintaining similar computational costs.
  • Latent-space analyses reveal representation differences not captured by classification metrics, highlighting strengths and limitations of individual methods.

Paper Type

  • Benchmarking

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Sarah M\\"uller', 'Philipp Berens']
  • Tags: ['shortcut-learning', 'feature-disentanglement', 'robustness', 'confounding', 'benchmark']

item.title: DCInject: Persistent Backdoor Attacks via Frequency Manipulation in Personal Federated Learning
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes DCInject, a novel frequency-domain persistent backdoor attack for personalized federated learning (PFL) that manipulates the DC (zero-frequency) component and replaces it with Gaussian samples.
  • Demonstrates high attack success rates (ASR) across image datasets (CIFAR-10/100, GTSRB, SVHN) while preserving clean accuracy, outperforming spatial-domain backdoor baselines.
  • Shows persistence of the backdoor under personalization and defenses (e.g., I-BAU), retaining much higher ASR than prior attacks and exposing vulnerabilities in PFL security assumptions.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Nahom Birhan', 'Daniel Wesego', 'Dereje Shenkut', 'Frank Liu', 'Daniel Takabi']
  • Tags: ['backdoor attack', 'federated learning', 'personalized federated learning', 'frequency-domain attack', 'adversarial security']

item.title: Reliable Abstention under Adversarial Injections: Tight Lower Bounds and New Upper Bounds
item.description:
Summary

  • Studies online learning under the adversarial injection (clean-label) model where i.i.d. examples are interspersed with adversarially chosen instances and the learner may abstain from predicting.
  • Proves a matching Omega(√T) lower bound for VC dimension 1, showing a fundamental separation between distribution-aware and distribution-agnostic regimes.
  • Introduces a potential-based framework driven by robust witnesses and two combinatorial dimensions (inference dimension and a new certificate dimension) to obtain distribution-agnostic upper bounds: Õ(T^{1-1/k}) in terms of inference dimension k and Õ(T^{2/3}) for 2D halfspaces.
  • Provides algorithmic defenses (abstention strategies and robust certification) and tight theoretical bounds against adversarial injection attacks in the clean-label setting.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Ezra Edelman', 'Surbhi Goel']
  • Tags: ['adversarial-injection', 'online-learning', 'robust-learning', 'abstention', 'theoretical-bounds']

item.title: BarrierSteer: LLM Safety via Learning Barrier Steering
item.description:
Summary

  • Presents BarrierSteer, an inference-time defense that embeds learned non-linear safety constraints into an LLM's latent space and steers responses away from unsafe trajectories.
  • Uses Control Barrier Functions (CBFs) to detect and prevent unsafe generations with efficient constraint merging, without fine-tuning or modifying base model weights.
  • Provides theoretical guarantees for applying CBFs in latent space and demonstrates empirical reductions in adversarial success rates and unsafe outputs across models and datasets.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Thanh Q. Tran', 'Arun Verma', 'Kiwan Wong', 'Bryan Kian Hsiang Low', 'Daniela Rus', 'Wei Xiao']
  • Tags: ['Safety Mechanism', 'Adversarial Defense', 'Inference-time Steering', 'Control Barrier Functions', 'Latent-space Constraints']

item.title: A Secure and Private Distributed Bayesian Federated Learning Design
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes a distributed federated learning (DFL) framework that integrates Bayesian local training with privacy preservation and Byzantine robustness via selective neighbor posterior exchanges.
  • Formulates neighbor selection as an optimization to minimize global loss under security and privacy constraints and analytically characterizes trade-offs between dynamic connectivity, Byzantine detection, privacy levels, and convergence speed.
  • Develops a fully distributed Graph Neural Network (GNN)-based Reinforcement Learning (RL) algorithm enabling devices to autonomously choose connection subsets using only local observations; simulations show improved robustness and efficiency with lower overhead.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Nuocheng Yang', 'Sihua Wang', 'Zhaohui Yang', 'Mingzhe Chen', 'Changchuan Yin', 'Kaibin Huang']
  • Tags: ['federated-learning', 'byzantine-robustness', 'privacy-preserving', 'distributed-security', 'graph-neural-networks']

item.title: DP-FedAdamW: An Efficient Optimizer for Differentially Private Federated Large Models
item.description:
Summary

  • Identifies challenges of using AdamW in Differentially Private Federated Learning (DPFL): amplified variance of second-moment estimator, DP-induced bias, and increased sensitivity to local overfitting/client drift.
  • Proposes DP-FedAdamW, an AdamW-based optimizer that stabilizes second-moment variance, removes DP-induced bias via an unbiased estimator, and aligns local updates to global descent to reduce client drift.
  • Provides theoretical guarantees: unbiased second-moment estimator, linearly accelerated convergence without heterogeneity assumptions, and tighter (ε,δ)-DP guarantees.
  • Empirically outperforms prior methods on language and vision Transformers and ResNet-18 (e.g., +5.83% on Tiny-ImageNet Swin-Base at ε=1); code available in appendix.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Jin Liu', 'Yinbin Miao', 'Ning Xi', 'Junkang Liu']
  • Tags: ['differential_privacy', 'federated_learning', 'optimizer', 'privacy_defense', 'convergence_analysis']

item.title: Expanding the Role of Diffusion Models for Robust Classifier Training
item.description:
Summary

  • Extends the use of diffusion models for adversarial robustness beyond synthetic data generation by leveraging their internal representations as auxiliary signals during adversarial training (AT).
  • Finds diffusion representations are diverse and partially robust; incorporating them into AT consistently improves classifier robustness and encourages more disentangled features.
  • Shows diffusion-generated data and diffusion representations play complementary roles in shaping robust representations.
  • Validates results with systematic experiments on CIFAR-10, CIFAR-100, and ImageNet.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Pin-Han Huang', 'Shang-Tse Chen', 'Hsuan-Tien Lin']
  • Tags: ['adversarial robustness', 'diffusion models', 'adversarial training', 'representation learning', 'synthetic data']

item.title: Rethinking LoRA for Privacy-Preserving Federated Learning in Large Models
item.description:
Summary

  • Identifies challenges when applying LoRA in differentially private federated learning (DPFL): gradient coupling between the two low-rank matrices, amplified DP noise, and sharpness of the aggregated model.
  • Proposes LA-LoRA (Local Alternating LoRA) to decouple gradient interactions and align client update directions, with theoretical convergence guarantees in noisy federated settings.
  • Empirical results show SOTA improvements under strict privacy budgets (e.g., substantial accuracy gains on Swin-B/Tiny-ImageNet and strong results on RoBERTa), demonstrating robustness of the method for both vision and language large models.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Jin Liu', 'Yinbin Miao', 'Ning Xi', 'Junkang Liu']
  • Tags: ['differential privacy', 'federated learning', 'privacy defense', 'LoRA / parameter-efficient fine-tuning', 'large models']

item.title: Unsupervised Anomaly Detection in NSL-KDD Using $\beta$-VAE: A Latent Space and Reconstruction Error Approach
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes an unsupervised intrusion/anomaly detection approach on the NSL-KDD network dataset using β-Variational Autoencoders (β-VAE).
  • Compares two detection strategies: measuring distances in the learned latent space to training projections versus using reconstruction error as an anomaly score.
  • Finds that exploiting latent space structure yields more effective classification/detection performance and discusses trade-offs of each method.
  • Provides experimental evaluation and analysis of advantages and limitations of latent-space vs. reconstruction-based detection in an unsupervised setting.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Dylan Baptiste (CRESTIC)', 'Ramla Saddem (CRESTIC)', 'Alexandre Philippot (CRESTIC)', 'Fran\\c{c}ois Foyer']
  • Tags: ['intrusion-detection', 'anomaly-detection', 'unsupervised-learning', 'variational-autoencoder', 'network-security']

item.title: Advantage-based Temporal Attack in Reinforcement Learning
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes Advantage-based Adversarial Transformer (AAT) to generate time-correlated adversarial perturbations against deep RL agents by modeling temporal dependencies in perturbation generation.
  • Introduces a multi-scale causal self-attention (MSCSA) to capture dependencies across different past time horizons and a weighted advantage mechanism to prioritize high-impact perturbations.
  • Evaluates AAT against baseline adversarial attacks on Atari, DeepMind Control Suite, and Google Football, reporting matched or improved attack effectiveness.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Shenghong He']
  • Tags: ['adversarial examples', 'reinforcement learning attacks', 'temporal attacks', 'transformer-based attack', 'attack evaluation']

item.title: LoMime: Query-Efficient Membership Inference using Model Extraction in Label-Only Settings
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes LoMime, a label-only membership inference attack that leverages model extraction to create a surrogate model S via active sampling, perturbation-based selection, and synthetic data, shifting queries to a one-time extraction phase.
  • Operates under strict black-box/label-only constraints and achieves membership inference accuracy within ±1% of the target model M while dramatically reducing per-sample query cost (extraction cost ≈ testing ~1% of training samples).
  • Evaluates effectiveness on benchmarks (Purchase, Location, Texas Hospital) and assesses standard defenses against label-only MIAs.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Abdullah Caglar Oksuz', 'Anisa Halimi', 'Erman Ayday']
  • Tags: ['membership-inference', 'model-extraction', 'label-only', 'privacy-attack', 'query-efficiency']

item.title: When World Models Dream Wrong: Physical-Conditioned Adversarial Attacks against World Models
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces PhysCond-WMA, a white-box adversarial attack that perturbs physical-condition channels (e.g., HDMap embeddings, 3D-box features) to produce semantic/logic/decision-level distortions in generative world-model outputs while keeping perceptual fidelity.
  • Proposes a two-stage optimization: (1) quality-preserving guidance that constrains reverse-diffusion loss, and (2) momentum-guided denoising that accumulates target-aligned gradients for temporally coherent semantic shifts.
  • Demonstrates effectiveness with modest perceptual degradation (≈+9% FID, ≈+3.9% FVD) and attack success rate up to 0.55; shows tangible downstream harm—training on attacked videos reduces 3D detection by ~4% and open-loop planning performance by ~20%.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information
